---
title: "Wierd .bak files keep reappearing in home"
date: 2008-02-22
forum: General Help
---

### Post by grogger13 on 2008-02-22
i keep getting these wierd .bak files appearing in my home directory and can't think of whats cuasing them.  There is usually about 30-40 of them when they come every couple of days. 
There titled "untitled*_MAS.bak" with * being the number. here is the first  of the files code
```
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<Worksheet>
<Version major="11" minor="0"/>
<Styles></Styles>
<Task>
</Task>
<Text-field selection-placeholder="false" executable="false" family="Times New Roman" opaque="false" foreground="[0,0,0]" superscript="false" placeholder="false" readonly="false" subscript="false" bold="false" italic="false" underline="false" background="[255,255,255]" size="12" linebreak="space" spaceabove="0" rightmargin="0" bullet="none" firstindent="0" bulletsuffix="" linespacing="0.0" pagebreak-before="false" leftmargin="0" alignment="left" initial="0" spacebelow="0"><Equation executable="false" input-equation="" display=""></Equation></Text-field>
</Worksheet>
```

---

### Post by rapiscan on 2008-02-22
.bak file is a standard extension used when creating a backup of something.  My guess is that you're running some application that is set to have auto-saved backups made as you work.  I'm not sure which application it is by looking at the file, but you can likely tweak the settings for said application to save in a specific directory (or turn off autosave altogether).

---
